meaning: adj.脾气暴燥的,易怒的n.[机]曲柄,奇想vt.[机]装曲柄vi.[机]转动曲柄
examples: The chauffeur got out to crank the motor.
He was idly turning a crank on a strange mechanism strapped to his chest.
He has a limited time to crank the reforms into action.
You may feel rushed, as these places crank out dozens of weddings every day.
Warmth, colors, antlers on the walls, an old-time crank phone like you saw in silent movies.
